import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

# Select features with potential outliers
# Currently we don't know which one happened that so I will take all numerical data
features = data.select_dtypes(include=[float64]).columns.to_list()

# Create box plost
fig = make_subplots(
    rows=1 , cols=len(features),
    subplot_titles=[f.replace("_", " ").title() for f in features]
)

colors = ['#667eea', '#f093fb', '#43e97b',
          '#667eea', '#f093fb', '#43e97b',
          '#667eea', '#f093fb', '#43e97b',
          '#667eea', '#f093fb', '#43e97b',
          '#667eea', '#f093fb', '#43e97b',
          '#667eea']

for idx, (feature, color) in enumerate(zip(features, colors), 1):
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Box(y=data[feature], name=feature, marker_color=color, boxmean='sd'),
        row=1, col=idx
    )

fig.update_layout(
    title_text='Outlier Detection - Box Plots',
    showlegend=False,
    height=400,
    width=125*len(features)
)

fig.show()

# IQR method for one feature
for feature in features:
    Q1 = data[feature].quantile(0.25)
    Q3 = data[feature].quantile(0.75)
    IQR = Q3 - Q1

    lower_bound = Q1 - 1.5 * IQR
    upper_bound = Q3 + 1.5 * IQR

    outliers = data[(data[feature] < lower_bound) | (data[feature] > upper_bound)]

    print(f"
{feature} - IQR Outlier Detection:")
    print(f"Q1: {Q1:.2f}, Q3: {Q3:.2f}, IQR: {IQR:.2f}")
    print(f"Bounds [{lower_bound:.2f}, {upper_bound:.2f}]")
    print(f"Outliers: {len(outliers)} ({len(outliers) / len(data) * 100 :.2f}%)")
    print(f"Outlier range: [{outliers[feature].min():.2f}, {outliers[feature].max():.2f}]")
